name: mltraq

metadata.name: mltraq
metadata.summary: Track and Collaborate on ML & AI Experiments.

documentation:
---

Track and Collaborate on ML & AI Experiments.

The open-source Python library for ML & AI developers to design, execute and share experiments. Track anything, stream, reproduce, collaborate, and resume the computation state anywhere. --- * **Documentation**: [https://www.mltraq.com](https://www.mltraq.com/) * **Source code**: [https://github.com/elehcimd/mltraq](https://github.com/elehcimd/mltraq) (License: [BSD 3-Clause](https://mltraq.com/license/)) * **Discussions**: [Ask questions, share ideas, engage](https://github.com/elehcimd/mltraq/discussions) * **Funding**: You can [star](https://github.com/elehcimd/mltraq) the project on GitHub and [hire me](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dallachiesa/) to make your experiments run faster ---

functions.name: mltraq.session.create_experiment
documentation: Create a new experiment bound to a new session. Shortcut to create a single experiment.

classes.name: mltraq.session.Session

methods.name: mltraq.session.Session.__init__
documentation:
Create a new session handler, with `url` as database URL and `ask_password`
triggering the interactive input of the password if True.
By default, an in-memory SQLite database is initialised.

Alternatively, an already instantiated `db` could be passed.

methods.name: mltraq.session.Session.__str__
documentation:
Return a string with an overview of the available experiments in the linked database.
Experiments that are not persisted are not visible.

methods.name: mltraq.session.Session.create_experiment
documentation:
Create a new experiment, binded to the database of this session:
- `name` is optional, a 6 alphanum hash of ID experiment is used if missing.
- `fields` is a dictionary loaded on `Experiment.fields`, with database persistence.

methods.name: mltraq.session.Session.ls
documentation: Returns a Pandas dataframe with the list of persisted experiments.

methods.name: mltraq.session.Session.load_experiment
documentation:
Loads a persisted experiment by `name` or `id_experiment`. If `pickle` is True, it will
attempt to reload the pickled Experiment object from database.
Unpickling Experiment objects is unsafe, but powerful.
Whenever possible, prefer the safe persistence of experiment states.

methods.name: mltraq.session.Session.persist_experiment
documentation:
Persist the experiment on the database linked by the session (as a copy), and return it.
The database considered is the one of the session, allowing us to copy experiments
between datasets. The new experiment will have a different UUID and its name will be retained,
if not specified otherwise via `name`.

If `name` is passed, it is used as name of the experiment to persist.

Parameter `if_exists` controls the behaviour in case of already existing experiment:
- If "fail", an exception will be triggered (default).
- If "replace", we experiment will be overwritten.
Instantiate a new session handler.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.serializer.UnsupportedCompressionCodec
documentation: Raised if an unsupported compression codec is requested.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.serializer.Serializer

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.serializer.Serializer.name
documentation: Return unique name/version of the serializer.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.serializer.Serializer.meta
documentation: Metadata about serializer configuration.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.serializer.Serializer.compress
documentation: Compress `data`.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.serializer.Serializer.decompress
documentation:
Try to decompress `data`, or return the original value
if the decompression procedure fails.
Abstract class for serializers.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.pickle.UnsafePickle
documentation: Raised if a potentially unsafe pickle ofcode is encountered.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.pickle.PickleSerializer.assert_safe
documentation:
Make sure that it is safe to unpickle the object, by checking the opcodes defining it.
No class/execution of code is allowed, only primitive types and containers (lists, dicts, tuples, sets).

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.pickle.PickleSerializer.serialize
documentation:
Serialize object:
1. Check safety of opcodes, if requested
2. Pickle
3. Compress, if requested

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.pickle.PickleSerializer.deserialize
documentation:
Deserialize bytes:
1. Attempt decompress
2. Check safety of opcodes
3. Unpickle

modules.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.pickle_opcodes_whitelist
documentation:
Dictionary generated and manually reviewed to mark opcodes as safe/unsafe.

We disallow all things class/reduce-related, allowing only the stack/memo management ops,
lists, dicts, tuples, sets, ints, floats, strings and bytes.
(eg, opcodes for buffers introduced in pickle5 are not allowed, as they're not useful
to encode the types listed above.)

# You can review the pickletools's implementation source code at:
# https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/Lib/pickletools.py

# Code to regenerate the dictionary:

--
from textwrap import indent
from pickletools opcodes

safe_opcodes = print("{")
for opcode in opcodes:
    comment = indent(f'opcode.code: {opcode.code} pcode.proto: {opcode.proto}
opcode.doc:
{opcode.doc}', '# ')
    print(f'"{opcode.name}" : False,
{comment}
')
print("}")
--

classes.name: mltraq.experiment.PickleNotFoundException
documentation: Exception raised if the pickled experiment is not found.

classes.name: mltraq.experiment.ExperimentAlreadyExists
documentation: Raised if we try to overwrite an existing experiment, with if_exists="fail".

classes.name: mltraq.experiment.ExperimentNotFoundException
documentation: Raised if we try to load an experiment that is not found in the database.

classes.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.__init__
documentation: Crete a new experiment linked to database `db`.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.__getstate__
documentation: Build state for pickling.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.__setstate__
documentation: Load state for unpickling.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.run
documentation: Returns a new Run object as temporary context.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.__str__
documentation: Compact representation of the experiment with its runs.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.add_runs
documentation:
Add runs to experiment. `params` is a dictionary containing lists and it's used to generate
parameters for experiments from its Cartesian product.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.merge_runs
documentation: Given a list of `runs`, merge them into the experiment.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.add_run
documentation: Add single run with a list of parameters.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.get_tablename
documentation:
Return the table name of the experiment.
The table name is constructed by concatenating the option prefix "database.experiment_tableprefix"
and the experiment name, sanitizing the resulting value to consier only characters [^0-9a-zA-Z].

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.load_runs
documentation: Load self.runs from database, using the serialization config as found in `meta`.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.copy_to
documentation:
Return a deep copy of the experiment, setting `name` and `db` if provided.
A new UUID for the copy of the experiment is always generated.
If the name of the experiment was the hashed UUID, in case of no `name`,
a `name` value aligned with the new UUID is generated from its hash.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.load_pickle
documentation:
Load an Experiment object from its pickle, provided a `db` and an experiment `name`.
This is *unsafe* and error-prone, you should prefer the offered persistence of `the fields attribute`.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.reload
documentation: Reload the experiment from database (discarding its current state).

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.load
documentation:
Load experiment `name` (or `id_experiment`) from `db`. If `pickle` is True, load
it from its pickled Experiment object (unsafe).

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.__call__
documentation: Shortcut to .execute(...).

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.execute
documentation:
Execute the experiment, by executing its runs:

- `steps` is an ordered list of functions applied to all runs in `self.Runs`
- `config` is passed to the runs (constant), accessible as `Run.config`
- `backend` and `n_jobs` are passed to joblib.Parallel
- `args_field` is the optional field name used to store/load both `Run.config` and `Run.params`

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.record
documentation: Build an SQLAlchemy ORM object from the existing Experiment object.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.get_metadata
documentation:
Return a tree-like dictionary with properties about the experiment and its
serialization strategy.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.persist
documentation:
Persist an experiment to the bound database, honoring `if_exists` the `store_unsafe_pickle`.
If `if_exists` is set to "fail" and the experiment exists, an exception will be triggered.
To overwrite existing experiments, set `if_exists` to "replace".

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.delete
documentation: Delete experiment from database, honoring `if_exists`.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.df
documentation:
Return a Pandas dataframe representing the experiment, flattening
the fields up to `max_level`.

methods.name: mltraq.experiment.Experiment.ls
documentation:
Return a Pandas Dataframe with a list of experiments referenced in table "experiments in `db`.
Three columns are returned:
- `id_experiment`: ID of the experiment (UUID)
- `name`: Name of the experiment
- `table_name` Table name with runs of the experiment

Class modeling a new experiment. Class attribute `model_cls` is the SQLAlchemy model representing the
"experiments" table in the database.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.Archive

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.Archive.from_bytes
documentation: Given a binary blob, create a new Archive instance binded to it.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.Archive.to_bytes
documentation: Return the binary blob representing the TAR file.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.Archive.create
documentation: Return an in-memory TAR archive.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.Archive.extract
documentation: Extracts the archive to `target` directory.
Creation of binary TAR binary blob archives and extraction to filesystem.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.__init__
documentation: Create a new linked archive.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.add_files
documentation:
Add files to an open archive file `fileobj`, from directory `src_dir`, using archive directory `arc_dir`,
including glob pattern `include`, excluding glob pattern `exclude`.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.create
documentation: Creates and stores the archive, returning its ArchiveStoreIO representation.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.get_target
documentation:
Get the target directory for the uncompressed TAR. If `target` is passed as
a parameter, it is returned with no changes. If missing, it defaults to the
concatenation of defaults for archivesture URL and relative path prefix.

The target directory is passed as first argument to Tarfile.extractall(...).

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.extract
documentation: Extracts the archive to `target` directory.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.getnames
documentation: Load archive and return a list with its archived file names.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.open
documentation: Return the Tarfile object pointing to the archive URL.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStoreIO.delete
documentation:
Delete directory `relative_path_prefix`, used to drop directory
associated to an experiment being deleted.
Handling of TAR files of directories.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStore.__init__
documentation: Lazily define an archive, without creating it.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStore.to_url
documentation:
If the archive doesn't exist yet, create it.
It returns the URL to the archive.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStore.from_url
documentation:
Extracts the archive from `url` and returns
an ArchiveStore object pointing to it.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.archivestore.ArchiveStore.get_target
documentation: Return the destination directory of the unarchived files

classes.name: mltraq.storage.models.Base
documentation: Define a base class for declarative class definitions.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.models.Experiment
documentation:
Model representing the index record of an experiment in the database:
- id_experiment: random UUID
- name: short 6-len alphanum hash of id_experiemnt by default
- meta: metadata about the serialized experiment
- fields: experiment state fields
- unsafe_pickle: pickled (unsafe) Experiment object
Using the recently introduced UUID type:
https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/type_basics.html#sqlalchemy.types.Uuid

classes.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStore

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStore.to_url
documentation: Stores the object, and returns a relative url pointing to it.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStore.from_url
documentation: Loads an object from an url.
Extends Bunch by providing a flexible storage manager for the values.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.__init__
documentation: Create a new linked object.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.get_filepath
documentation: Get pathname prefix, given an url.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.delete
documentation:
Delete directory `relative_path_prefix`, used to drop directory
associated to an experiment being deleted.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.serialize_write
documentation: Serialize object to file.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.get_next_pathname_url
documentation: Generate the next pathname and url to store a new resource.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.write
documentation:
Create a new linked object "/.../optional relative path prefix/..., write to it,
and return a DataStore instance.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.read
documentation: Return the contents of the linked object.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.__getstate__
documentation: Create state for pickling. Only attributes in `__state__` are considered.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastore.DataStoreIO.__setstate__
documentation: Set state for unpickling.

Handling of data storage outside database.
Currently, supporting only filesystem storage.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.serialize
documentation: Serialize object, using the preferred serializer.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize object, using the preferred serializer.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.meta
documentation:
Get dictionary describing the preferred serialization strategy, and
serializer name/version.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.unsafe_pickle
documentation: Pickle object, compression is optional.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.unsafe_unpickle
documentation:
Unpickle object, without limiting to safe opcodes.
Used to unpickle complete Experiment objects.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.meta_runs
documentation:
Return dictionary with metadata about the runs persistence:
- table name for experiment
- numer of runs
- fields and their python types
- serialized/non-serialized columns

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serialization.runs_to_sql
documentation:
Prepare a Runs object for experiment `id_experiment`,
with metadata `meta`, to be stored in SQL.
It returns a Pandas dataframe and dtype(s) to use in the insert.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.sanitize_table_name
documentation: Sanitize the table name from the experiment name, allowing only lowercase alphanum characters.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.pandas_query
documentation:
Evaluate an SQL query on the database `session`, returning the result as a
Pandas dataframe. If `tqdm_total` is passed, it will be used as hint
on the count of returned rows for the progress bar.

Option "database.query_read_chunk_size" controls how many rows are read per chunk.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.next_uuid
documentation:
Return the next UUID to use.

If "reproducibility.sequential_uuids" is set to True:
    - Return an ascending, sequential list of UUIDs, with increments of `inc`.
    - `seq` is used as static variable and should not be set directly.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.chunker
documentation:
Given a `list` of items, return a list of lists of items,
where each sub-list is up to a certain chunk `size`.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.tqdm_chunks
documentation:
Renders a progress bar, working on chunks of work.
Functions to execute are fetched from `iterator`,
`total` is the expected count to reach.

Functions return a pair (size, result), with
`size` being accumulate to reach `total`.

It returns a list of the accumulated `result` values.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.hash_uuid
documentation:
Create a 6-alphanum hash of `value`, which can be
either a string representing an UUID or an UUID object.
If uuid1 == uuid2, hash_uuid(uuid1) == hash_uuid(uuid2).

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.query_count
documentation: Returns the number of rows returned if executing `query`.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.database.normalize_query
documentation: Normalize SQL query to an executable statement.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.__init__
documentation:
Initialize connection to a new database, with connection `url`,
asking interactively for a password if `ask_password` is True.
Options `echo` and `pool_pre_ping` are passed to SQLAlchemy.
(if None, their defaults do apply.)

If `lazy` is True, do not initialize the connection to the database.
Useful to handle unpickling of Database objects.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.copy
documentation:
Return an independent thread-safe copy of the object,
linked to the same URL and using the same options.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.init_url
documentation:
Initialize the URL to the database, handling defaults,
special cases and interactive passwords.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.__getstate__
documentation: Create state for pickling. Only attributes in `__state__` are considered.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.__setstate__
documentation: Set state for unpickling.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.pandas_to_sql
documentation:
Insert a Pandas dataframe `df` as a new database table `name`.
If `if_exists` == "replace", it overwrite existing tables.
If `if_exists` == "fail", it will trigger an exception if the table exists.
`dtype` passes the types to consider, if any.

Progress bar with tqdm.
Option "database.query_write_chunk_size" controls the number of rows to write per chunk.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.get_table_names
documentation: Return table names.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.drop_table
documentation: Drop table it it exists, returning True. Returns False otherwise.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.query_table
documentation: Read the complete table `name` from db

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.get_table_columns
documentation: Given a table `name`, return its SQLAlchemy columns.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.vacuum
documentation: Vacuum the database. This operation makes the database more compact and performant.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.database.Database.query
documentation: Query database with `query` (a string) and return result as a Pandas dataframe.

This class is the entry point for all things SQLAlchemy, and it represents the link
to the database for experiments. It uses the SQAlchemy 2.0 API interface.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.codelog.codelog_step
documentation: Track code of `steps` as a `code` field of the run.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.ensure_bytes
documentation:
Ensure that the type of `data` is indeed `bytes`, as a safety
precaution to ensure that we are not writing in the serialized
blob any unexpected object type.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.encode_magic_key
documentation:
Encode the complex object, using as key MAGIC_KEY to identify
the dictionary as a serialized complex object.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.decode_magic_key

classes.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.UnsupportedObjectType
documentation: No other type is supported. If other types are encountered,
an exception UnsupportedObjectType is rised.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.DataPakSerializer

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.DataPakSerializer.serialize
documentation:
Serialize an object:
1. Encode it
2. Serialize it with Pickle
3. Compress it, if requested

methods.name: mltraq.storage.serializers.datapak.DataPakSerializer.deserialize
documentation:
Deserialize a bytes object:
1. Decompress it, if compressed
2. Unpickle it, making sure that only safe opcodes are used
3. Decode it
Class that implement the DataPak serialization format.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.chdir.step_chdir
documentation: Change current directory to `path`.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.chdir.chdir
documentation:
Change the current directory. If `path` is None,
it changes the current directory on the worker process
to the main process directory.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.init_sequences.step_init_sequences
documentation: Initialize empty sequences to the run.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.init_sequences.init_sequences
documentation: It returns a step function that initializes one or more empty sequences to the run.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.sleep.step_sleep
documentation: Sleep for `duration` seconds.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.sleep.sleep
documentation: It returns a callable step that sleeps for `duration` seconds.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.init_fields.step_init_fields
documentation: Initialize fields in the run.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.init_fields.init_fields
documentation: It returns a callable step that initializes a set of fields.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.drop_fields.step_drop_fields
documentation: Drop fields in the run.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.drop_fields.drop_fields
documentation: It returns a callable step that drops a set of fields.

functions.name: mltraq.steps.nothing.nothing
documentation: Does nothing, empty step.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.resolve_inet_address
documentation: Given an INET `address` eg "x.yz", resolve it to an IP address.

functions.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.datastream_server
documentation: Starts a datastream server, called by the CLI tool.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.TooBigException
documentation: Handling of messages too big to be sent by the specified DataStreamKind.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new server, linking it to the specified `db`. The `db` object is expected to not be actively
used by other threads.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.thread_main
documentation:
Handle the incoming messages. This is executed as the thread main function and it blocks.
It starts the thread handled by `DatabaseWriter`.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.recv_handler
documentation:
Handle incoming messages, reading them, deserializing them,
and passing them to `func`.

It sets the event `self.ready`, signaling that the thread is now
ready to read new messages from the socket.

The function can be interrupted by setting the event `self.terminate`.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.update_stats
documentation: Update stats.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.start
documentation:
Start the server, `blocking` if set to True.
It starts the thread running `process_messages()`.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.cleanup
documentation: Executed by the thread to clean up its state before exiting.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamServer.stop
documentation: Requests the termination of the thread. It blocks until the thread terminates.

Server handling incoming messages, using two threads:

- DataStreamServer: Handling of incoming messages, adding them to a queue of deserialized messages.
- DatabaseWriter: Consuming batches of deserialized messages, adding them to the database.

The two activities are decoupled s.t. inserts in the database do not impact the reception of new messages.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamClient

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamClient.__init__
documentation: Link the client to a `run`.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamClient.send
documentation: Serialize `obj` and send it.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamClient.send_sequence
documentation:
Send `record` of Sequence, s.t. the state of the remote object can be updated.
`record` contains two keys, `field_name` and `record`.

The inner `record` contains at least keys `idx` and `timestamp`.
`idx` can be used to identify lost messages.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamClient.cleanup
documentation: Cleaning up of the client, closing the socket and updating the stats.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DataStreamClient.update_stats
documentation: Update the stats.

Sends messages to the server. No threads. It uses UNIX/INET datagrams, relying
on the system buffer. If messages are sent too frequently, some of them will be lost.

classes.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter.__init__
documentation: Create a new instance linked to `db`, which is then binded to a session to load/persist experiments.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter.thread_main
documentation: Check for new messages, and if any, process them.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter.process_batch
documentation: Process all messages, persisting the updated experiments.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter.start
documentation: Start a new DatabaseWriter thread.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter.stop
documentation: Request the termination of the thread.

methods.name: mltraq.storage.datastream.DatabaseWriter.cleanup
documentation: Called by the thread to clean up its internal state before termination.

Handling of database writes. Whenever a new experiment is seen in the messages, it tries to load
its state from the database, and it adds new records to the specified run/sequence.
Regularly, it persists the updated experiment.

functions.name: mltraq.run.normalize_steps
documentation: Normalise `steps` to always return a list of callables (which might be empty).

classes.name: mltraq.run.RunException
documentation: Created to communicate (not raised) a raised exception within a step function.

classes.name: mltraq.run.Run

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.__init__
documentation:
Create a new run, with:
- `id_run`: ID of the run (UUID)
- `steps`: Functions to apply to the run object to execute the run
- `config`: Fixed parameters for all runs
- `params`: Variable parameters, might be different for each run
- `fields`: State of the `run`

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.__getstate__
documentation: Create state of the run for pickling. Only attributes in `__state__` are considered.

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.__setstate__
documentation:
Set state of the run for unpickling.

Note: __init__() is not called when unpickling an instance, this is why we need
to initialise self.vars and self.steps (which are not part of __state__).

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.execute_func
documentation: Return callable that executes self.run(steps=steps, config=config, options=options).

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.execute
documentation:
Executes the `steps` callables on `self`, after:
1. Setting the `config`
2. Updating thevglobal options to reflect `options`
3. Initializing the numpy/random seeds to a seed that is unique to the run ID, honoring
the option "reproducibility.random_seed".

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.clear_after_execution
documentation: Clear attributes that should not be accessed after the execution of steps.

methods.name: mltraq.run.Run.df
documentation: Return a Pandas dataframe representing the fields of the run, flattending `run.fields` up to `max_level`.
Instance of an experiment with fixed and variable parameters.

functions.name: mltraq.opts.options
documentation: Returns singleton object of options.

classes.name: mltraq.opts.Options
documentation: Default options.

functions.name: mltraq.runs.normalize_runs
documentation: Normalise `runs` to always return a list of Run objects (which might be empty).

classes.name: mltraq.runs.RunsException
documentation: Raised in case of exceptions within runs.

classes.name: mltraq.runs.Runs

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.__init__
documentation: Initialise the runs.

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.add
documentation: Add run(s).

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.first
documentation: Return a run, no guarantees on which one.

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.next
documentation: Add a new run and return it.

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.df
documentation:
Return a pandas DataFrame representation of the collection of runs,
flattending run.fields up to `max_level`.

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.handle_args_field
documentation: Save/reload to/from run.fields[name] both run.params and run.config in a dictionary.

methods.name: mltraq.runs.Runs.execute
documentation:
Given an existing collection of runs, execute `steps` on them, considering `config`, `backend`, and `n_jobs`.
`backend` and `n_jobs` are passed to joblib.Parallel.
`args_field` is the optional field name used to store/load both `Run.config` and `Run.params`.
Dict handling of a collection of Run objects.

functions.name: mltraq.cli.print_df
documentation: Pretty prints a `df` Pandas DataFrame using tabulate.

functions.name: mltraq.cli.parse_args
documentation: Parse arguments, and return them.

functions.name: mltraq.cli.main
documentation: Entry point for the CLI tool.

functions.name: mltraq.cli.experiment_stats
documentation: Returns a Pandas DataFrame describing the contents of `experiment`.

functions.name: mltraq.job.parallel
documentation: Execute a list of callables, `tasks`, in parallel, returning their return values as a list.

classes.name: mltraq.job.ProgressParallel

methods.name: mltraq.job.ProgressParallel.__init__
documentation:
Create a new progress bar, with `total` number of tasks,
`args`/`kwargs` additional parameters to pass to the joblib.Parallel constructor.

methods.name: mltraq.job.ProgressParallel.__call__
documentation: Execute tasks in parallel with progress bar.

methods.name: mltraq.job.ProgressParallel.print_progress
documentation: Our chance to update the progress bar once tasks complete.
Manage progress bar monitoring of parallel execution of tasks.

classes.name: mltraq.job.Job

methods.name: mltraq.job.Job.__init__
documentation:
Prepare a new job to execute: `tasks` are callables to evaluate, `n_jobs` and `backend`
are passed to joblib.Parallel. If not set, default values from options are used.
Using ‘n_jobs=1’ enables to turn off parallel computing for debugging.

methods.name: mltraq.job.Job.execute
documentation: Execute the job.
Manage the execution of tasks as a parallelized job.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.text.stringify
documentation:
Convert object `d` to JSON string, capping its approximate length to `max_len`.
Useful to report/log stats of objects without risking too long outputs.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.text.UUIDEncoder
documentation: Extension of JSONEncoder to support UUIDs.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.reproducibility.seed_ctx
documentation:
Context with a randomness seed for both NumPy and random module.
If a `seed` is not provided, the default from options is used.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysmon.get_stats
documentation: Return a dictionary representing the system usage.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.sysmon.SystemMonitor

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sysmon.SystemMonitor.start
documentation: Start monitoring thread.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sysmon.SystemMonitor.thread_main
documentation: Thread entry point.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sysmon.SystemMonitor.stop
documentation: Requests the termination of the thread. It blocks until the thread terminates.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sysmon.SystemMonitor.cleanup
documentation: Called for clean up. Currently, nothing to do.
Class managing the thread that captures the system stats.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.json.dumps
documentation:
Same as json.dumps, but it supports also additional types
as provided by `UUIDEncoder`.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.json.MyEncoder
documentation: JSON encoder supporting `UUID` and `type` types.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.exceptions.exception_message
documentation:
Option "execution.exceptions.compact_message" controls if
either a compact or a complete stack trace is reported.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.exceptions.complete_exception_message
documentation: Returns a string with the complete stack trace report.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.exceptions.report_filename
documentation:
Return the filename, limiting it to the basename if requested.
Used to anonymize experiment outputs, minimizing disclosures.
Impacting only compact exception messages.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.exceptions.compact_exception_message
documentation:
Construct a compact string representing the position anc code
that caused the exception.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.exceptions.validate_type
documentation:
Validate the type of `value` to be `expected_type`, if provided.
Otherwise, return `value` with no checks.
TODO: avoid use of Any.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.exceptions.ExceptionWithMessage
documentation: Exception with a .message attribute

functions.name: mltraq.utils.logging.init_logging
documentation: Initialize the logging to stdout, setting it to `level_name` or the level specified in the options.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.logging.logging_ctx
documentation: Temporarily configure the logging `level_name` and `log_format`.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.is_tty
documentation: Returns true if code is running inside a terminal.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.is_colab
documentation: Returns true if code is running inside a Google Colab.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.is_pyodide
documentation: Returns true if code is running inside Pyodide (JupyterLite, ...).

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.is_notebook
documentation: Returns true if code is running inside a Notebook (IPython).

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.try_callable
documentation: Call the function. In case of exceptions or None, return an empty string.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.try_module
documentation:
Call the function from a module. If missing, exceptions
or None, return an empty string. If not callable, return
it as a string.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.sysenv.get_sysenv_info
documentation: Return a summary of the Python environment, including installed packages, Python, platform, system versions.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.web.fetch
documentation:
Fetch URL with requests, saving it to `pathname` or a random one if not provided.

The function returns a Bunch with attributes `url` with the input URL, `name` with
the guessed name of the file, and `pathname` with the local saved copy pathname.

If `pathname` is not provided, a temporary pathname is used.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.enums.enforce_enum
documentation:
Convert string `x` to an enum value for type `enum_type`, or do nothing in case of enums.
An invalid value results in a raised exception.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.frames.reorder_columns
documentation:
Given a dataframe, enforce the initial columns in the order
`ordered_columns`, appending the remaining ones after sorting them.
Why sorting: this ensures that, with queries like *, the order of returned columns is consistent.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.fs.glob
documentation: Wrapper for glob.glob that implements the root_dir parameter for Python 3.9 that lacked it.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.fs.tmpdir_ctx
documentation:
Create and enter a temporary directory, deleted as the context exits.
if `shutdown_joblib_workers` is true, terminate the workers created y joblib.
This ensures that the current directory is honored by all newly created workers.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.fs.chdir_ctx
documentation:
Enter `dirname` directory, and leave it
as the context returns.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.fs.globs
documentation:
Enumerate files matching the glob patterns in the `include` string list, excluding
matches in `exclude` string list, inside directory `src_dir`.

functions.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.add_option_argument
documentation:
Add option --option to cli parameters, to change the
value of package options via command line.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.instance
documentation: Get instance of the class.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.print
documentation: Pretty print the values tree.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.get
documentation:
If `prefer` is provided (not None), return it right away without lookup.
Elif return option or dictionary representing the subtree at `path`, is set
Elif `otherwise` is provided, return it.
Else raise a KeyError.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.set
documentation: Sets the value of `path` to value.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.copy_from
documentation: Copy the values of the options from a dictionary of `values`.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.copy_values
documentation: Returns a deepcopy of the `values`.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.reset
documentation:
Rset the `path` value to its default.
If `path` is None, it resets all options.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.set_argument_options
documentation: Given a list of Option objects, set them.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.flatten
documentation: Return a dictionary of KEY:VALUE pairs, no nested dictionaries.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.BaseOptions.ctx
documentation:
Provides context manager for temporary options.
E.g., options.ctx({'a.b': 123}).

Handle options as a tree of dictionaries.
Following singletorn pattern.
This class must be extended with a preoper initialization
for `default_values`, it should not be used directly.
Options can be addressed with dotted strings.
E.g., "group1.value".

classes.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.ArgumentOption

methods.name: mltraq.utils.base_options.ArgumentOption.__init__
documentation:
Given an `option` string in the format NAME=VALUE,
checks the validity of `name` and `value` and sets them.

It represents an option passed as an argument,
used for input validation.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.Bunch

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.Bunch.__init__
documentation:
Same constructor of dict type. Additionally,
if input is None, it returns an empty dict.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.Bunch.dict_to_bunch_deep
documentation: Deep-conversion of a dict to a Bunch.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.Bunch.bunch_to_dict_deep
documentation:
Deep-conversion of a Bunch to a dict.
We return dict objects without looking for Bunch values inside them.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.Bunch.cartesian_product
documentation:
Cartesian product of arguments:

Given a list of key:value pairs, values are treated as lists
and the Cartesian product of all possible combinations of
values form each item is returned.
Ordered dictionary whose elements can be accessed as object attributes.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchEvent

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchEvent.__init__
documentation: Same constructor of Bunch, with initializations.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchEvent.clear_triggers
documentation: Initialize triggers, removing existing ones, if any.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchEvent.on_setattr
documentation: Add trigger to call `func` with parameter `key`, `value` on setattr events.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchEvent.on_getattr
documentation: Add trigger to call `func` with parameter `key`, `value` on getattr events.

Bunch with function triggers on events "on_setattr", "on_getattr".
Useful to implement assertions, debugging procedures and alerts.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchStore

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchStore.__init__
documentation:
Initialize and load the key-value store. The bunch is serialized/deserialized to `pathname` (or its
default value "bunchstore.pathname"), appending `suffix` if provided.
In case of read-only use cases, specify `read_only=True` to avoid unnecessary writes.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchStore.read
documentation: Load inner Bunch from file (if available).

methods.name: mltraq.utils.bunch.BunchStore.write
documentation:
Overwrite BunchStore file on filesystem, using a temporary file
to avoid concurrency issues.
Basic key-value store on filesystem for a single Bunch object.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.InvalidKey
documentation: Triggered in case a column with a reserved key is used.

classes.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.__init__
documentation: Initialize the sequence to the empty sequence, or with `data` if not None.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.__len__
documentation: Return the number of rows of the sequence.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.__getstate__
documentation: Create state of the Sequence for pickling. Only attributes in `__state__` are considered.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.__setstate__
documentation:
Set state of the Sequence for unpickling.

Note: __init__() is not called when unpickling an instance, this is why we need
to initialise other attributes which are not part of __state__.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.append
documentation: Append a new dictionary of tracked values.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.stream_recv
documentation:
Add `record` as received by the stream.
The record must contain keys `idx`, `timestamp`,
as set by .stream_send(...).

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.size
documentation: Flush and return number of rows in Pandas dataframe.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.clear
documentation: Clear the sequence.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.flush
documentation: Flush .data contents to .frame, concatenating existing and new dataframes.

methods.name: mltraq.utils.sequence.Sequence.df
documentation: Convert the sequence to a Pandas dataframe, with `timestamp` column.

Class representing a growing sequence of tracked attributes, with `idx` and `timestamp` (microsecond precision).

In case of data streams:
- There might be missing records, which can be identified by gaps in the `idx` values
- Rows are not guaranteed to be in ascending `idx` order
